page.content: The following table presents a comprehensive analysis of the relationship between various treatment markers and behavioral changes in subjects exposed to cocaine and 7-NI.

lines.text: The following table presents a comprehensive analysis of the relationship between various treatment markers and behavioral
changes in subjects exposed to cocaine and 7-NI.

page.content: Specifically, the metrics of interest include the hazard ratios (HR) for both LRC and overall survival (OS), as well as the associated p-values, which provide insight into the statistical significance of the findings.

cells.cell_text: N = 7 II; N = 12 III; N = 2 IV; N = 3 VI

page.content: Among the markers analyzed, PD-L1 demonstrates an HR of 0.67 (95% CI: 0.34–3.50) for LRC, with a p-value of 0.78, indicating a lack of significant association with treatment outcomes.

lines.text: Among the markers analyzed, PD-L1 demonstrates an HR of 0.67 (95% CI: 0.34–3.50) for LRC, with a p-value of 0.78,
indicating a lack of significant association with treatment outcomes.

page.content: Conversely, the OS analysis shows an HR of 1.21 (95% CI: 0.51–2.80), suggesting a potential trend that merits further investigation, although the p-value of 0.65 reinforces the absence of statistical significance. The mean age of participants was 47 years, with a range from 32 to 64, highlighting the demographic diversity within the study. Notably, the observed behavioral changes, particularly decreased locomotor activity, underscore the impact of substance exposure on neurological outcomes, warranting further exploration in future research endeavors.
